subject: Gaming
title: Sunk Cost Fallacy in Gaming
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Sunk Cost Fallacy in Gaming.

hook.text: You've spent 200 hours on a game you no longer enjoy. But quitting feels like wasting those hours.

buildup.text: The sunk cost fallacy says we overvalue past investments. Time already spent shouldn't affect future fun.

discovery.text: MMOs exploit this. Your character, gear, and progress feel too valuable to abandon—even when bored.

twist.text: Free-to-play games add money on top of time. Quitting means losing both your hours and your purchases.

climax.text: Recognizing sunk cost helps players quit games that stopped being fun—and find joy elsewhere.

punchline.text: Two hundred hours invested. But time spent doesn't mean fun ahead.

quiz.question: What is the sunk cost fallacy in gaming?
options: Continuing to play because of time already invested, not current enjoyment
Spending too much money on a new game
Buying games during sales you never play
